get.summary:
Returns the session reports, the number of remaining reports and
a link to request the further reports if the remaining number is > 0.
All parameters are nullable, i.e. optional. We detect the type of the token
implicitly by getting AccountId and CompanyId out of the token.
In the backend, we filter the reports depending on which value is set.
Only one of these values must and may be set.
Reports are sorted by end date descending, i.e. newest sessions first.
With each call, at most 1000 session reports are returned.
<para>GET api/v1/reports/connections</para>

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: LocalSystemUser that created the session

parameters.name: userid
parameters.description: AccountId to which the session belongs

parameters.name: groupid
parameters.description:
GroupId that is used to filter session reports.
            Only reports to devices within this group will be returned.
            Special case: 0 means "no group", so only reports to unnamed devices are returned.

parameters.name: devicename
parameters.description:
ServerName. Used to filter reports. Only
            reports to devices with this name will be returned.

parameters.name: deviceid
parameters.description:
TeamViewerId to filter sessions.
            Is a string as it is in the DB scheme.

parameters.name: from_date
parameters.description: Returned reports shall start at >= this date

parameters.name: to_date
parameters.description: Returned reports shall end before this date

parameters.name: offset_id
parameters.description:
Used to partition the results. If there are more than 1000 results for
            a call, there is a link to the further results that contains the ParticipantSessionId
            of the last session. With this, we can exactly determine which results
            have already been received. All reports after the row with ParticipantSessionId
            will be returned when the results are ordered by end date descending.
            The row with the ID itself will be skipped.

parameters.name: has_code
parameters.description:
If set to true will filter out reports without a session
            code. If false, it will filter out reports with a session code. Will not filter
            out anything if the parameter is left out.

parameters.name: session_code
parameters.description: If specified only returns connection reports for specified session code

parameters.name: support_session_type
parameters.description: If specified only returns connection reports of the given session type - valid values are "RemoteConnection" / "1" and "RemoteSupportActive" / "2" and "RemoteSupportActiveSdk" / "3"

parameters.name: sessionReportFeatures
parameters.description: If specified, only reports which have the specified feature will be returned.

put.summary: Changes properties of a managed group
put.description:
Modify the attributes of a managed group using its designated "id."
You can provide the group's "name" (alias) to enact changes or/and provide the policy parameter to update or add a policy.
The policy parameter is consisted from the policy id and the policy type (TeamViewerClient policy: 1, Monitoring policy: 4, PatchManagement policy: 5)
of the policy to be updated or added to the group.
            
The current account needs "GroupAdministration" and "PolicyAdministration" manager permissions on the group.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The managed group ID.

requestBody.description: Properties to change.

400.description: The request does not include any changes to the managed group.

403.description: Insufficient permissions to modify the specified managed group.

404.description: The managed group with the given identifier cannot be found.

delete.description:
Marks the managed group with the given "id" as deleted.
It will disappear from the list of managed groups of all associated
managers. Devices in the group will see the deletion marker and
remove themselves from the group as soon as they get online.
            
The current account needs "GroupAdministration" manager
permissions on the group.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The managed group ID.

403.description: Insufficient permissions to remove the specified managed group.

404.description: The managed group with the given identifier cannot be found.

parameters.description: The managed group ID.

put.summary: Changes multiple managers of a managed group
put.description:
Changes permissions of multiple managers (specified in the request
body) on the managed group with the given "id".
            
It is not possible to take away the "ManagerAdministration"
permission from all managers of a managed group.
At least one manager must remain with that permission.
            
The current account needs "ManagerAdministration" manager
permissions on the group.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The managed group ID.

400.description:
The request either exceeds the maximum of number of managers (100),
does not include any changes
or attempts to demote the last manager with `ManagerAdministration` permissions.

403.description: Insufficient permissions to change managers on the given managed group.

404.description: The managed group with the given identifier cannot be found.

put.summary: Changes properties of a device
put.description:
Modify the attributes of a managed device using its designated "id."

You can provide one or more of the following device properties to change

(1) the device's "name" (alias) to enact changes,
(2) the TeamViewer policy change (add or update), by setting either the "teamviewerPolicyId",
or the "managedGroupId" to inherit the TeamViewer Policy from a managed group to which the device is associated, and
(3) permissionInheritanceType [0: NotAllowed, 1: Allowed]

The current account needs the following permissions on the device for each action:
"DeviceAdministration or InheritDeviceAdministration" for alias change,
"PolicyAdministration or InheritPolicyAdministration" for policy change,
"DeviceAdministration or InheritDeviceAdministration" for permissionInheritanceType change
            
The change of 'permissionInheritanceType' is NOT allowed (1) to Non-CompanyAdmin accounts and
(2) when the device belongs to a different company than the session's company.
            
The change of 'permissionInheritanceType' is allowed when (1) the device does not have a company manager or
(2) the device belongs to the same company as the account.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The management ID of the device.

requestBody.description: The device properties to change.

400.description: The request does not include any changes.

403.description: Insufficient permissions to change properties of the given device.

404.description: The device with the given management identifier cannot be found.

put.summary: Changes multiple managers of a device

Changes permissions of multiple managers (specified in the request
body) on the directly managed device with the management ID "id".
            
It is not possible to take away the "ManagerAdministration"
permission from all managers of a device.
At least one manager must remain with that permission.
            
The current account needs "ManagerAdministration" manager
permissions on the device.

The values for the `permissions` property can be one or more of:
0 = ManagerAdministration,
1 = GroupAdministration,
2 = DeviceAdministration,
3 = PolicyAdministration,
4 = DeviceDescription,
5 = EasyAccess,
6 = SoftwareUpdate,
7 = CustomFieldsAdministration,
